jobs.title: Corporate Tax Intern – Finance Department (Geneva)
jobs.description:
General Info

- Department: Corporate Tax

- Work time Percentage: 100%

- Location: Geneva

- Duration 8-9 months – part of mandatory curricular internship and with tripartite agreement.

Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

Job Description

We are looking for a competent university student to gain hands-on experience in a dynamic financial services environment. If you have the skills and enthusiasm we are looking for and are excited about the opportunity to enhance our Finance - Corporate Tax functions, please apply today.

## Key areas of responsibilities

- Tax audits preparation including analysing any potential mismatches

-
GloBE/Pillar II implementation of the dedicated IT tool

-
Extraction and analysis of accounting and other data that has an impact on VAT

-
Other useful tasks/extractions from Power BI

-
TP Genie (IT tool) implementation – intra-group financial flows

-
TP (Transfer Pricing) analysis – reconciliations and Globus/HFM checks

-
Tracking unpaid invoices

-
Tracking unsigned intra group agreements

-
Any ad-hoc question or urgency which would arise during the assignment

Requirements and Qualifications

- University student, preferably in Corporate Finance.

- Fluent in English, French is a plus.

-
Excellent analytical and research skills with detail orientation

-
Good knowledge of accounting and bookkeeping procedures

-
Strong ability to organize and prioritize tasks

-
Reporting and numeracy skills

-
Good at meeting deadlines and solving problems

-
Ability to work well independently as well as the ability to work well with stakeholders

Our Values

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

-
Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

jobs.title: Finance Data Analytics & Power BI Internship
jobs.description:
## Our Company EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice. EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation. ## Our Purpose and Mission Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future. We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value. ## What are we looking for?
We are seeking a talented and motivated university student to join our finance team as a Finance Data Analytics & Power BI Intern for a 7-month contract. The successful candidate will play a critical role in leveraging Power BI to improve reporting, controls, and operational efficiency within the finance department. Responsibilities will be centred on utilizing Power BI to enhance the accuracy, transparency, and efficiency of financial data reconciliation, with a particular focus on complex revenue streams such as forex, derivatives, and structured products.

Key Responsibilities & Achievements ## Power BI Reconciliation - Assist in the reconciliation of net other income revenue, using Power BI to cross-validate accounting data from multiple sources (statutory and consolidation levels), ensuring data integrity and consistency. - Support the automation of reconciliation processes, reducing manual effort and minimizing errors in financial reporting. ## Dashboard Development - Collaborate with technical Power BI specialists to design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports tailored for financial data reconciliation and control. - Enhance real-time visibility for finance teams, enabling faster identification and resolution of reconciliation discrepancies. ## Process Improvement - Conduct in-depth analysis of existing reconciliation workflows and lead discussions with key stakeholders to define and implement a target operating model, integrating Power BI as a core tool for reconciliation and reporting. - Project manage the transformation of reconciliation processes, from requirements gathering to implementation and user training. ## Internal Controls & Compliance - Strengthen the Internal Control Signoff (ICS) process by embedding automated reconciliation checks within Power BI reports, supporting audit readiness and compliance with IFRS standards. - Contribute to the update of the Accounting Manual, reflecting improved reconciliation practices and Power BI integration. ## Stakeholder Engagement - Act as a bridge between finance, IT, and management teams, ensuring alignment of reconciliation objectives and smooth adoption of new Power BI tools. ## Qualifications - University student, preferably in Finance. - Project management or Finance competencies (including basic knowledge of derivatives). - Fluent in English. - Rigorous, result-driven, and good relationship skills are required qualities for this position. - Able to work from the Geneva Office, reporting to a UK – London based line manager. This is a unique opportunity for a competent university student to gain hands-on experience in a dynamic financial services environment. If you have the skills and enthusiasm we are looking for and are excited about the opportunity to enhance our finance functions through advanced data analytics and reporting, please apply today. ## INFORMATION The internship will last 7 months starting asap and his based in Geneva
requirements: University student, preferably in Finance.

Fluent in English.

Able to work from the Geneva Office, reporting to a UK – London based line manager.

bullets: Strengthen the Internal Control Signoff (ICS) process by embedding automated reconciliation checks within Power BI reports, supporting audit readiness and compliance with IFRS standards.
Contribute to the update of the Accounting Manual, reflecting improved reconciliation practices and Power BI integration.

sections.heading: Stakeholder Engagement
bullets: Act as a bridge between finance, IT, and management teams, ensuring alignment of reconciliation objectives and smooth adoption of new Power BI tools.

sections.heading: INFORMATION
paragraphs: The internship will last 7 months starting asap and his based in Geneva

jobs.title: Apprendistato Impiegato/a di commercio AFC Banca
jobs.description:
Stai finendo la scuole media e sei interessato a diventare un professionista in banca?

Se questo è il tuo desiderio l'Apprendistato di Commercio AFC ramo Banca di EFG Bank è quello che fa per te.

Lavorerai in delle sedi principali svizzere di EFG Bank in centro a Lugano da agosto 2026 fino a agosto 2029 quando avrai ottenuto l'Attestato Federale di Capacità.

Durante i tre anni farai esperienze in team di lavoro diversi che operano a livello nazionale e internazionale e si occupano ad esempio di Private Banking, Compliance, Credit Cards, Central Filing, Credit, Business Risk Management, Asset Management, etc.

Oltre alla formazione in azienda, in cui sarai seguito da un tutor a te dedicato in ogni team, frequenterai la scuola professionale commerciale (CPC) di tuo interesse e i corsi presso il centro competenze bancarie (CYP) assieme a tutti gli apprendisti di commercio ramo banca del Cantone.

EFG Bank supporta e incoraggia lo svolgimento della maturità professionale integrata per garantire una formazione il più possible completa al termine dell'apprendistato.

Durante il percorso potrai sviluppare diverse competenze professionali, tra le quali:

- analisi del mercato finanziario e del credito

- conoscenza dei prodotti bancari

- elaborazione e verifica di documenti

- pianificazione delle scadenze e gestione delle priorità

- utilizzo dei sistemi informatici finanziari

## Requisiti

- Passione per l’economia e il mondo bancario

- Licenza di scuola media con ottimi risultati

- Spiccate capacità relazionali e facilità ad adattarsi a diversi scenari lavorativi

- Buone conoscenze informatiche

- Conoscenza della lingua inglese

Se sei motivato, grazie per allegare alla candidatura online: una lettera di motivazione, il CV, le tre più recenti pagelle scolastiche e la licenza di scuola media.

descriptionByLocale.de:
Sind Sie mit der Mittelschule fertig und daran interessiert, Profi in der Bank zu werden?

Wenn dies Ihr Wunsch ist, ist die AFC Trade Apprentice Bank of EFG Bank das, was sie für Sie tut.

Sie werden von August 2026 bis August 2029 an den Hauptbüros der EFG Bank im Zentrum von Lugano arbeiten, wenn Sie das Bundeszertifikat für Kapazität erhalten.

Während der drei Jahre werden Sie verschiedene Arbeitsgruppen auf nationaler und internationaler Ebene erleben, zum Beispiel Private Banking, Compliance, Credit Cards, Central Filing, Credit, Business Risk Management, Asset Management, etc.

Neben der Ausbildung in der Firma, in der Sie von einem Tutor für Sie in jedem Team, Sie werden die kommerzielle professionelle Schule (CPC) Ihres Interesses und die Kurse im Bank-Kompetenzzentrum (CYP) zusammen mit allen Business-Auszubildenden Zweigbank des Kantons folgen.

Die EFG Bank unterstützt und fördert die Entwicklung einer integrierten Berufsbildung, um eine möglichst vollständige Ausbildung am Ende der Lehrlingsausbildung zu gewährleisten.

Während des Kurses können Sie verschiedene berufliche Fähigkeiten entwickeln, darunter:
- Analyse des Finanz- und Kreditmarktes
- Kenntnis der Bankprodukte
- Verarbeitung und Überprüfung von Dokumenten
- Terminplanung und Prioritätsmanagement
- Einsatz von IT-Systemen

Anforderungen
- Leidenschaft für die Wirtschaft und Bankwelt
- Highschool-Lizenz mit hervorragenden Ergebnissen
- Erklären Sie relationale Fähigkeiten und einfache Anpassung an verschiedene Arbeitsszenarien
- Gute Computerkenntnisse
- Englischkenntnisse

Wenn Sie motiviert sind, dank der Anbindung an die Online-Anwendung: ein Schreiben der Motivation, CV, die drei neuesten Schulberichte und die Mittelschule Lizenz.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Est-ce que vous terminez le collège et que vous voulez devenir un professionnel à la banque?

Si c'est votre souhait, l'AFC Trade Apprentice Bank of EFG Bank est ce qu'elle fait pour vous.

Vous travaillerez dans les principaux bureaux suisses de la Banque EFG au centre de Lugano à partir d'août 2026 jusqu'à août 2029 lorsque vous obtiendrez le certificat fédéral de capacité.

Au cours des trois années, vous vivrez différentes équipes de travail travaillant aux niveaux national et international, par exemple banque privée, conformité, cartes de crédit, dépôt central, crédit, gestion des risques d'entreprise, gestion d'actifs, etc.

En plus de la formation dans l'entreprise, dans laquelle vous serez suivi par un tuteur qui vous sera dédié dans chaque équipe, vous assisterez à l'école professionnelle commerciale (CPC) de votre intérêt et les cours au centre de compétence bancaire (CYP) avec tous les apprentis d'affaires de la succursale du canton.

EFG Bank soutient et encourage le développement d'une formation professionnelle intégrée pour assurer une formation aussi complète que possible à la fin de l'apprentissage.

Pendant le cours, vous pouvez développer différentes compétences professionnelles, notamment:

- analyse du marché financier et du crédit

- connaissance des produits bancaires

- traitement et vérification des documents

- planification des échéances et gestion des priorités

- utilisation des systèmes informatiques financiers

Exigences
- Passion pour l'économie et le monde bancaire
- Licence d'études secondaires avec d'excellents résultats
- Expliquer les capacités relationnelles et la facilité d'adaptation aux différents scénarios de travail
- Bonne connaissance informatique
- Connaissance de l'anglais

Si vous êtes motivé, merci de joindre à l'application en ligne: une lettre de motivation, CV, les trois derniers rapports scolaires et la licence du collège.

jobs.title: Operational Risk and Advisory IT and Information Security Specialist
jobs.description:
## Job Description

The position of Operational Risk and Advisory Specialist is within the Operational Risk Function, a critical component of the second line of defense. This role is responsible for providing comprehensive oversight of operational risk activities, with a strong emphasis on new technologies, information security, and cyber risks. The Operational Risk function engages in advisory, risk assessment, analysis, and reporting to the Bank’s governing bodies.

## Main Responsibilities

-  Development, Implementation, and Execution of Risk Activities:

- Enhance the risk management framework in relation to risk assessment and advisory, particularly in relation to new technologies, information security and cyber risks.

- Collaborate with business units to integrate advanced risk management practices into their operations.

-  Risk Assessment and Analysis:

- Conduct ad hoc risk assessments and analyses, especially concerning information security and cyber threats, to identify potential issues and propose efficient mitigating measures.

- Perform incident analysis to determine root causes, identify mitigating actions, and ensure follow-up.

-  Advisory Support:

- Provide advisory support to Business Units, including reviewing the Internal Control Framework with a focus IT and Information Security in the way they support the sustainable business development of the Bank

- Offer expert advice on risk mitigation strategies, particularly related to information security and emerging technologies.

-  Reporting and Communication:

- Report and present risk assessment results to the Risk Control leadership group and other relevant stakeholders, with a focus on cyber risk and technological vulnerabilities.

- Prepare detailed risk reports and presentations for executive review.

-  Digital Solutions Deployment:

- Actively participate in deploying new digital solutions to support the implementation of the internal control system globally, ensuring these solutions address information security and cyber risk concerns.

## Skills and Experience

- University degree in business administration, economics, science, information security, or a related field.

- 10 years of working experience, including consulting or audit at a leading professional services firm and/or in a comparable position at a market-leading bank.

- Strong understanding of core banking systems, end-to-end transaction processing, and cybersecurity principles.

- Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.

- Excellent analytical skills and a structured approach to problem-solving and reporting.

- Exceptional communication skills in English, both written and spoken.

- Capacity to work autonomously and as part of a global team in a fast-paced environment.

- Experience with new technologies and a deep understanding of information security and cyber risk management.

## Our values

-  Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges as well as seeking continuous improvement.

-  Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.

-  Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence.

-  Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.

-  Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.
requirements: University degree in business administration, economics, science, information security, or a related field.
10 years of working experience, including consulting or audit at a leading professional services firm and/or in a comparable position at a market-leading bank.
Strong understanding of core banking systems, end-to-end transaction processing, and cybersecurity principles.
Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.
Excellent analytical skills and a structured approach to problem-solving and reporting.
Exceptional communication skills in English, both written and spoken.
Capacity to work autonomously and as part of a global team in a fast-paced environment.
Experience with new technologies and a deep understanding of information security and cyber risk management.

jobs.title: Technology & Cyber Information Security Risk Analyst (Temporary)
jobs.description:
## Job Description

The Technology & Cyber Information Security Risk Analyst will be part of the Operational Risk team. We are looking for a passionate IT risk, cyber and information security to cover a global role located in Switzerland.

Location: Geneva, Zurich, Lugano.

## Main responsibilities

- Oversee the effective and consistent implementation and maintenance of the risk and control management framework globally.

- Contribute to thematic risk and control assessments on IT security issues, cyber risk, and threat analysis through collaboration with other internal stakeholders.

- Perform process-level walkthroughs and control testing to enable the identification and assessment of risks and controls.

- Provide expertise, make recommendations for improvements to steer adherence to internal regulations, regulatory requirements and industry best practices, and monitor their implementation.

- Manages relationships with key stakeholders within the First Line of Defence.

- Stays abreast and advises the Bank on innovative business and technology trends in risks and controls.

Skills and experience

- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Cyber Security, Information Technology, or equivalent.

- Professional certification, e.g. Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP), Information Security Manager, (CISM), Information Systems Auditor (CISA), Risk and

- Information Systems Control (CRISC) or equivalent.

- At least 3-5 years of experience in banking IT platforms, operational processes and IT controls, or in consulting/audit with a leading professional services firm; a combination of both is considered an advantage.

- Excellent communication skills in English, both verbal and written. Knowledge of German, French, or Italian is a plus.

- In-depth understanding of key technology and information security concepts relevant to banking. In particular, strong knowledge of IT organisation, business processes and systems (e.g. IT Security, data management, architecture and planning, life cycle management and regulatory matters).

- Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.

- Self-motivated, diligent, highly organized, able to prioritize and multi-task, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

- Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, able to work autonomously and as part of a global team.

## Our values

- Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges as well as seeking continuous improvement.

- Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results.

- Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence.

- Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset.

- Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. jobs.title: EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions - Human Resources - Lugano
jobs.description:
We are looking for a young talent to join our EFG Graduate Programme – Corporate Functions path in Lugano , with a focus on Human Resources .

In this role, you will gain hands-on experience and valuable insight into the key areas that support and drive the success of EFG.

You will have the opportunity to work closely with our HR team, participate in talent acquisition, employee development, and performance management initiatives, and contribute to projects that shape EFG’s people strategy. This experience will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of how Human Resources enables EFG to deliver outstanding service and maintain its leadership in private banking.

How the EFG Graduate Programme works?

Starting in September 2026 and over a period of 18 months, the EFG Graduate Programme offers a challenging and exciting opportunity for recent graduates. Tailored on individual skills, interests, and competencies, the programme provides an ideal opportunity to obtain wide-ranging experience in an international and fast-growing private banking group:

- You will start with a 2-week onboarding in Switzerland to discover EFG Mission, Vision and Culture and start building your professional network.

- During the onboarding you will have the chance to attend specific trainings aimed at enhancing key competencies and communication skills.

- Continuous learning opportunities to ensure you develop both personally and professionally to achieve full potential.

- Buddy and Mentor to accompany you throughout the programme.

- Truly global, we have positions based in Miami, London, Luxembourg, Geneva, Zurich, Lugano, Singapore and Hong Kong.

Could you be one of our future graduates?

We value diversity and we do not require specific academic majors or industry experience, but look for different mix of background, experiences and skills.

## Our graduates are brilliant and enthusiastic talents, so we expect

- Bachelor or Master degree (completed in 2025 or on track to complete it) with outstanding academic results in a field with affinity with the selected professional path.

- Excellent English (any other language will be an advantage)

- International mindset

- Strong analytical, problem solving and organisational skills

- Forward thinking and able to suggest new ways of working to improve efficiency and competitiveness

- Self-disciplined, committed and with strong work ethics

- Adaptable and able to work collaboratively in a team environment with people from different backgrounds

- Client-oriented

- Proactive self-starter with intellectual curiosity

What will you get?

- Dynamic work experience by rotating through different teams and business units

- Experience an enriching international network

- Opportunity to build expertise in your chosen field and contribute to the EFG Culture

- Continuous development and learning opportunities on both technical and soft skills

The recruitment process for this position will begin in April 2026 and will include several interviews.

descriptionByLocale.it:
Siamo alla ricerca di un giovane talento per unirsi al nostro Programma di Laurea EFG – Funzioni Corporate percorso in Lugano, con un focus sulle Risorse Umane. In questo ruolo, acquisirai esperienza pratica e una comprensione valutabile delle aree chiave che supportano e guidano il successo di EFG. Avrai l'opportunità di lavorare in stretta collaborazione con il nostro team HR, partecipare all'acquisizione di talenti, allo sviluppo degli impiegati e alla gestione delle prestazioni, e contribuire a progetti che definiscono la strategia delle persone di EFG. Questa esperienza ti fornirà una comprensione completa di come le Risorse Umane consentono a EFG di offrire un servizio eccellente e mantenere la leadership nel private banking. Come funziona il Programma di Laurea EFG? Iniziando a settembre 2026 e per un periodo di 18 mesi, il Programma di Laurea EFG offre un'opportunità stimolante e affascinante per i recenti laureati. Personalizzato sulle abilità, gli interessi e le competenze individuali, il programma offre un'opportunità ideale per ottenere un'esperienza vasta e variegata in un gruppo di private banking internazionale e in rapida crescita:
- Inizierai con un'iscrizione di 2 settimane in Svizzera per scoprire la Missione, la Visione e la Cultura di EFG e iniziare a costruire la tua rete professionale.
- Durante l'iscrizione avrai la possibilità di partecipare a specifici corsi di formazione mirati a migliorare le competenze chiave e le abilità di comunicazione.
- Opportunità di apprendimento continuo per assicurarti di sviluppare sia personalmente che professionalmente per raggiungere il tuo pieno potenziale.
- Buddy e Mentore per accompagnarti durante il programma.
- Veramente globale, abbiamo posizioni basate a Miami, Londra, Lussemburgo, Ginevra, Zurigo, Lugano, Singapore e Hong Kong. Potresti essere uno dei nostri futuri laureati? Valutiamo la diversità e non richiediamo specifici corsi di laurea o esperienze nel settore, ma cerchiamo una diversa miscela di background, esperienze e competenze. I nostri laureati sono talenti brillanti e entusiasti, quindi ci aspettiamo:
- Laurea o Master (completato nel 2025 o in corso di completamento) con risultati accademici eccezionali in un campo affine al percorso professionale selezionato.
- Inglese eccellente (qualsiasi altra lingua sarà un vantaggio)
- Mindset internazionale
- Abilità analitiche, di risoluzione dei problemi e organizzative forti
- Pensiero proattivo e in grado di suggerire nuove modalità di lavoro per migliorare l'efficienza e la competitività
- Auto-disciplinato, impegnato e con un forte senso del dovere
- Adattabile e in grado di lavorare in un ambiente di lavoro collaborativo con persone di diversi background
- Orientato al cliente
- Autonomo e proattivo con curiosità intellettuale. Che cosa otterrai?
- Esperienza lavorativa dinamica facendo rotazione attraverso diverse squadre e unità di business
- Esperienza di rete internazionale arricchente
- Opportunità di costruire competenze nel tuo campo scelto e contribuire alla Cultura di EFG
- Opportunità di sviluppo e apprendimento continuo sia sulle competenze tecniche che sulle competenze soft. Il processo di selezione per questa posizione inizierà a aprile 2026 e includerà diverse interviste.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Wir suchen einen jungen Talent für unser EFG-Graduiertenprogramm – Unternehmensfunktionen-Weg in Lugano, mit Schwerpunkt auf Personalwesen. In dieser Rolle wirst du praktische Erfahrung und wertvolle Einblicke in die Schlüsselfunktionen gewinnen, die den Erfolg von EFG unterstützen und antreiben. Du wirst die Möglichkeit haben, eng mit unserem Personalteam zusammenzuarbeiten, an der Talentakquisition, der Mitarbeiterentwicklung und der Leistungsbeurteilung teilzunehmen und an Projekten mitzuarbeiten, die die Strategie der Menschen von EFG definieren. Diese Erfahrung wird dir einen umfassenden Einblick in die Rolle des Personalwesens bei der Bereitstellung eines herausragenden Dienstleistungsangebots und der Führung in der Privatbankwirtschaft geben. Wie funktioniert das EFG-Graduiertenprogramm? Ab September 2026 und über einen Zeitraum von 18 Monaten bietet das EFG-Graduiertenprogramm eine herausfordernde und spannende Gelegenheit für jüngere Absolventen. An individuellen Fähigkeiten, Interessen und Kompetenzen angepasst, bietet das Programm eine ideale Gelegenheit, um eine breite Palette an Erfahrungen in einem internationalen und wachsenden Privatbankengruppen zu sammeln:
- Du beginnst mit einer 2-wöchigen Einarbeitung in der Schweiz, um die Mission, Vision und Kultur von EFG kennenzulernen und deine berufliche Netzwerke aufzubauen.
- Während der Einarbeitung hast du die Möglichkeit, spezifische Schulungen zu besuchen, um Schlüsselkompetenzen und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten zu verbessern.
- Kontinuierliche Lernmöglichkeiten, um sicherzustellen, dass du sowohl persönlich als auch beruflich voll entwickelt bist, um dein volles Potenzial zu erreichen.
- Buddy und Mentor, die dich während des Programms begleiten.
- Tatsächlich global, haben wir Positionen in Miami, London, Luxemburg, Genf, Zürich, Lugano, Singapur und Hongkong. Könntest du einer unserer zukünftigen Absolventen sein? Wir schätzen Vielfalt und fordern keine spezifischen akademischen Abschlüsse oder Branchenerfahrungen, sondern suchen eine Vielfalt an Hintergründen, Erfahrungen und Fähigkeiten. Unsere Absolventen sind brillante und begeisterte Talente, also erwarten wir:
- Bachelor- oder Master-Abschluss (abgeschlossen 2025 oder in der Pipeline) mit hervorragenden akademischen Leistungen in einem Bereich, der mit dem ausgewählten Berufsweg zusammenhängt.
- Ausgezeichnete Englischkenntnisse (jede andere Sprache ist ein Vorteil)
- Internationale Denkweise
- Starke analytische, Problemlösungs- und Organisationsfähigkeiten
- Proaktives Denken und in der Lage, neue Arten der Arbeit zu vorschlagen, um Effizienz und Wettbewerbsfähigkeit zu verbessern
- Selbstdiszipliniert, engagiert und mit starkem Sorgfaltspflichtgefühl
- Anpassungsfähigkeit und in der Lage, in einem Teamumfeld mit Menschen aus verschiedenen Hintergründen zusammenzuarbeiten
- Kundeorientiert
- Proaktiver Selbststarter mit intellektueller Neugier. Was wirst du erhalten?
- Dynamische Arbeitserfahrung durch Rotation durch verschiedene Teams und Geschäftseinheiten
- Erfahrung eines erweiterten internationalen Netzwerks
- Gelegenheit, Expertise in deinem ausgewählten Bereich zu entwickeln und zum EFG-Kulturbezug beizutragen
- Kontinuierliche Entwicklung und Lernmöglichkeiten auf technischen und weichen Fähigkeiten. Der Auswahlprozess für diese Position beginnt im April 2026 und umfasst mehrere Interviews.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Nous recherchons un jeune talent pour rejoindre notre Programme de recrutement EFG – Fonctions d'entreprise en voie de Lugano, avec un focus sur les Ressources humaines. Dans ce rôle, vous acquérez une expérience pratique et une compréhension précieuse des domaines clés qui soutiennent et impulsent le succès d'EFG. Vous aurez l'opportunité de travailler en étroite collaboration avec notre équipe RH, de participer à l'acquisition de talents, au développement des employés et à la gestion des performances, et de contribuer à des projets qui définissent la stratégie des personnes d'EFG. Cette expérience vous fournira une compréhension complète de la manière dont les Ressources humaines permettent à EFG de fournir un service exceptionnel et de maintenir sa leadership dans la banque privée. Comment fonctionne le Programme de recrutement EFG ? À partir de septembre 2026 et sur une période de 18 mois, le Programme de recrutement EFG offre une opportunité stimulante et passionnante pour les jeunes diplômés. Adapté aux compétences, aux intérêts et aux compétences individuels, le programme offre une opportunité idéale pour acquérir une expérience large et variée dans un groupe de banque privée internationale et en croissance rapide :
- Vous commencez par une formation de 2 semaines en Suisse pour découvrir la mission, la vision et la culture d'EFG et pour commencer à construire votre réseau professionnel.
- Pendant la formation, vous avez la possibilité de suivre des formations spécifiques pour améliorer vos compétences clés et vos compétences de communication.
- Opportunités de formation continue pour vous assurer de développer à la fois personnellement et professionnellement pour atteindre votre plein potentiel.
- Buddy et Mentor pour vous accompagner tout au long du programme.
- Vraiment global, nous avons des postes à Miami, Londres, Luxembourg, Genève, Zurich, Lugano, Singapour et Hong Kong. Pourriez-vous être l'un de nos futurs diplômés ? Nous valorisons la diversité et ne demandons pas de spécialisations académiques ou d'expérience professionnelle spécifiques, mais nous recherchons une diversité de profils, d'expériences et de compétences. Nos diplômés sont des talents brillants et enthousiastes, donc nous attendons :
- Diplôme de licence ou de master (obtenu en 2025 ou en cours de validation) avec des résultats académiques exceptionnels dans un domaine lié au chemin professionnel sélectionné.
- Excellentes compétences en anglais (toute autre langue est un atout)
- Mentalité internationale
- Compétences analytiques, de résolution de problèmes et organisationnelles solides
- Pensée proactive et capable de suggérer de nouvelles façons de travailler pour améliorer l'efficacité et la compétitivité
- Auto-discipliné, engagé et avec un fort sens du devoir
- Adaptable et capable de travailler dans un environnement de travail collaboratif avec des personnes de divers horizons
- Orienté client
- Proactif et autonome avec une curiosité intellectuelle. Qu'est-ce que vous obtiendrez ?
- Expérience de travail dynamique en faisant la rotation dans différentes équipes et unités de business
- Expérience d'un réseau international enrichissant
- Opportunité de développer des compétences dans votre domaine choisi et de contribuer à la culture d'EFG
- Opportunités de développement et d'apprentissage continu sur les compétences techniques et les compétences douces. Le processus de recrutement pour cette position commencera en avril 2026 et comportera plusieurs entretiens.

summary:
We are looking for a young talent to join our EFG Graduate Programme – Corporate Functions path in Lugano, with a focus on Human Resources.

In this role, you will gain hands-on experience and valuable insight into the key areas that support and drive the success of EFG.

You will have the opportunity to work closely with our HR team, participate in talent acquisition, employee development, and performance management initiatives, and contribute to projects that shape EFG’s people strategy. This experience will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of how Human Resources enables EFG to deliver outstanding service and maintain its leadership in private banking.

How the EFG Graduate Programme works?

Starting in September 2026 and over a period of 18 months, the EFG Graduate Programme offers a challenging and exciting opportunity for recent graduates. Tailored on individual skills, interests, and competencies, the programme provides an ideal opportunity to obtain wide-ranging experience in an international and fast-growing private banking group:

  • You will start with a 2-week onboarding in Switzerland to discover EFG Mission, Vision and Culture and start building your professional network.
  • During the onboarding you will have the chance to attend specific trainings aimed at enhancing key competencies and communication skills.
  • Continuous learning opportunities to ensure you develop both personally and professionally to achieve full potential.
  • Buddy and Mentor to accompany you throughout the programme.
  • Truly global, we have positions based in Miami, London, Luxembourg, Geneva, Zurich, Lugano, Singapore and Hong Kong.

Could you be one of our future graduates?

We value diversity and we do not require specific academic majors or industry experience, but look for different mix of background, experiences and skills.

Our graduates are brilliant and enthusiastic talents, so we expect:

  • Bachelor or Master degree (completed in 2025 or on track to complete it) with outstanding academic results in a field with affinity with the selected professional path.
  • Excellent English (any other language will be an advantage)
  • International mindset
  • Strong analytical, problem solving and organisational skills
  • Forward thinking and able to suggest new ways of working to improve efficiency and competitiveness
  • Self-disciplined, committed and with strong work ethics
  • Adaptable and able to work collaboratively in a team environment with people from different backgrounds
  • Client-oriented
  • Proactive self-starter with intellectual curiosity

What will you get?

  • Dynamic work experience by rotating through different teams and business units
  • Experience an enriching international network
  • Opportunity to build expertise in your chosen field and contribute to the EFG Culture
  • Continuous development and learning opportunities on both technical and soft skills

The recruitment process for this position will begin in April 2026 and will include several interviews.

jobs.title: PB Support Specialist Americas Geneva
jobs.description:
## Job Description

The role bridges business needs with regulatory standards and ensures the smooth execution of regulatory, compliance, and operational processes for activities in the Americas region.

## Main responsibilities

- Responsible for the output of the PB Support work area within the discipline, with a focus on implementing and executing strategies and ensuring alignment with Swiss standards and regulatory expectations

- Support the Client Relationship Officers in proactively identifying, managing and mitigating risks arising from day-to-day business activities, applying advanced professional expertise and conceptual thinking to complex cases

- Perform due diligence on prospective and existing client relationships, ensuring a consistent approach and applying frameworks, processes, and routines aligned with regulatory expectations in a timely and qualitative manner

- Approve funds and securities transfers, credit granting, overrides and transactions involving sanctions sensitivity, within delegated authority, ensuring decisions reflect the standards and specifications applicable to the business

- Distribute market knowledge and best practices among Client Relationship Officers, Client Service Officers and Account Managers, defining and embedding standard around which others will operate

- Ensure timely completion and remediation of pending follow-ups relating to client lifecycle activities, being accountable for delivery of assigned processes/programmes impacting the department

- Approve clients and business introducers new accounts, oversee KYC lifecycle and periodic reviews within the authority delegated by HoPB, ensuring oversight and compliance standards are met through in-depth professional expertise

- Act as a key senior partner to Front Office and control Functions and as a key link between FLOD and SLOD, ensuring consistent communication and escalation paths, and networking with key contacts across multipe levels and teams internally and externally

- Review account openings, CIP modifications and periodic reviews for the local PBS teams across the Region, while providing guidance to strengthen their understanding of requirements and control standards. Through continuous support and best practice sharing, provide guidance to more junior members of the team and help develop the capability to perform these reviews independently.

## Skills and Experience

- Lead medium-sized projects/BAU assignments to improve control standards documentation quality checks and risk mitigation measures

- Provide guidance and deliver presentation sessions to Front Office teams on risk topics, control obligations and regulatory expectations, frequently advising on complex matters related to the area of expertise

- Conduct risk assessment, control checks and thematic reviews assigned by Senior Management (Line Manager, HOPB & RBH), applying judgement and innovation where issues may be unclearly defined

- Prepare and present high-risk and sensitive cases on behalf of HoPB to CAC committee

- Resolve audit issues by coordinating with stakeholders to implement corrective actions, being accountable for the delivery of remediation plans impacting the department and the business unit

- Support the local PBS team in enhancing their operational processes, providing guidance on control requirements, workflow improvements, and risk-mitigation measures, including developing solutions and frameworks with a longer-term view, to ensure alignment with Swiss standards

## Our Values

-  Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

-  Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

-  Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence

-  Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

-  Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

sections.heading: Our Company
paragraphs: EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

sections.heading: Our Purpose and Mission
paragraphs: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

jobs.title: Client Support Officer
jobs.description:
General Info

-
Work time Percentage : 100%

-
Location : St Moritz

## Job Description

To support our growing activities in St. Moritz, we are looking for a Client Service Officer who will provide high-quality administrative and client service support to our Client Relationship Officers (CROs), while also contributing to the smooth running of our front office reception and cashier operations.

This role is ideal for a service-oriented, hands-on professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international private banking environment and who is motivated to take ownership, act proactively and deliver an outstanding client experience.

## Main Responsibilities

## 1. Client and CRO Support

-
Support clients and CROs in their daily business activities.

-
Maintain active client contact in written and spoken German and English.

-
Provide top-quality service to clients through regular and prompt contact by phone and email.

-
## Execute client account openings, closures and updates, including

-
Conducting internet searches and basic due diligence checks.

-
Collecting and verifying corroborative documentation.

-
## Prepare and follow up on client visits, including

-
Preparing account documentation and reviewing documents.

-
Organising account statements and other supporting material.

-
## Execute and control client instructions, such as

-
Stock market transactions.

-
Payments and transfers.

-
Foreign currency trades.

-
## Perform various administrative and organisational tasks, including

-
Client reviews and periodic checks.

-
Follow-up on missing or pending documents.

-
Delivery and monitoring of hold mail.

-
## Prepare marketing and client materials, such as

-
Presentations and pitchbooks.

-
Investment proposals & portfolio reviews.

-
Actively promote paperless accounts and e-banking usage to clients.

-
Ensure full compliance with the Code of Conduct and all internal regulations (e.g. Cross Border, AML, Clear & Clean Desk Policy, Absence Management).

## 2. Reception and Front Office Support

-
Welcome and assist clients upon arrival, ensuring a professional and warm first impression.

-
## Manage reception duties, including

-
Answering and directing phone calls.

-
Handling appointments and coordinating meeting room bookings.

-
Prepare and serve beverages to clients and ensure a high standard of hospitality.

-
Ensure meeting rooms and client areas are tidy, well-presented and adequately equipped at all times.

-
Handle incoming and outgoing mail and deliveries, ensuring timely distribution and proper documentation.

## 3. Cashier and Branch Support

-
## Support daily cashier operations, including

-
Processing client deposits and withdrawals.

-
Handling basic cash and payment transactions in line with internal guidelines.

-
Collaborate closely with CROs and other team members to ensure smooth branch operations.

-
Support marketing and client events, including preparation, logistics and on-site assistance.

## Skills and experience

-
Education and Professional Background

-
Commercial apprenticeship in a bank or similar banking-related education.

-
Further education in the financial sector is an advantage.

-
Minimum 3 years of experience in a private banking or intermediary environment.

-
Skills

-
## Good understanding of financial instruments, including

-
Equities, bonds, structured products and derivatives.

-
Solid knowledge of Compliance and AML topics relevant to private banking.

-
Confident user of Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Word).

-
Familiarity with banking systems and front-office tools is an asset.

-
Strong client service orientation with excellent interpersonal skills.

-
High level of reliability, discretion and integrity.

-
Ability to cope with pressure and tight deadlines, with a flexible approach to working hours when required.

-
Comfortable working in a small branch environment, taking on a broad range of tasks (from client administration to reception and cashier support).

-
Proficient in written and spoken German and English (essential).

-
Italian is a strong plus; any additional languages are an advantage.

## Our Values

-
Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

-
Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

-

descriptionByLocale.en:
General Info

-
Work time Percentage : 100%

-
Location : St Moritz

## Job Description

To support our growing activities in St. Moritz, we are looking for a Client Service Officer who will provide high-quality administrative and client service support to our Client Relationship Officers (CROs), while also contributing to the smooth running of our front office reception and cashier operations.

This role is ideal for a service-oriented, hands-on professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international private banking environment and who is motivated to take ownership, act proactively and deliver an outstanding client experience.

## Main Responsibilities

## 1. Client and CRO Support

-
Support clients and CROs in their daily business activities.

-
Maintain active client contact in written and spoken German and English.

-
Provide top-quality service to clients through regular and prompt contact by phone and email.

-
## Execute client account openings, closures and updates, including

-
Conducting internet searches and basic due diligence checks.

-
Collecting and verifying corroborative documentation.

-
## Prepare and follow up on client visits, including

-
Preparing account documentation and reviewing documents.

-
Organising account statements and other supporting material.

-
## Execute and control client instructions, such as

-
Stock market transactions.

-
Payments and transfers.

-
Foreign currency trades.

-
## Perform various administrative and organisational tasks, including

-
Client reviews and periodic checks.

-
Follow-up on missing or pending documents.

-
Delivery and monitoring of hold mail.

-
## Prepare marketing and client materials, such as

-
Presentations and pitchbooks.

-
Investment proposals & portfolio reviews.

-
Actively promote paperless accounts and e-banking usage to clients.

-
Ensure full compliance with the Code of Conduct and all internal regulations (e.g. Cross Border, AML, Clear & Clean Desk Policy, Absence Management).

## 2. Reception and Front Office Support

-
Welcome and assist clients upon arrival, ensuring a professional and warm first impression.

-
## Manage reception duties, including

-
Answering and directing phone calls.

-
Handling appointments and coordinating meeting room bookings.

-
Prepare and serve beverages to clients and ensure a high standard of hospitality.

-
Ensure meeting rooms and client areas are tidy, well-presented and adequately equipped at all times.

-
Handle incoming and outgoing mail and deliveries, ensuring timely distribution and proper documentation.

## 3. Cashier and Branch Support

-
## Support daily cashier operations, including

-
Processing client deposits and withdrawals.

-
Handling basic cash and payment transactions in line with internal guidelines.

-
Collaborate closely with CROs and other team members to ensure smooth branch operations.

-
Support marketing and client events, including preparation, logistics and on-site assistance.

## Skills and experience

-
Education and Professional Background

-
Commercial apprenticeship in a bank or similar banking-related education.

-
Further education in the financial sector is an advantage.

-
Minimum 3 years of experience in a private banking or intermediary environment.

-
Skills

-
## Good understanding of financial instruments, including

-
Equities, bonds, structured products and derivatives.

-
Solid knowledge of Compliance and AML topics relevant to private banking.

-
Confident user of Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Word).

-
Familiarity with banking systems and front-office tools is an asset.

-
Strong client service orientation with excellent interpersonal skills.

-
High level of reliability, discretion and integrity.

-
Ability to cope with pressure and tight deadlines, with a flexible approach to working hours when required.

-
Comfortable working in a small branch environment, taking on a broad range of tasks (from client administration to reception and cashier support).

-
Proficient in written and spoken German and English (essential).

-
Italian is a strong plus; any additional languages are an advantage.

## Our Values

-
Accountability : Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

-
Hands-on : Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

-
Passionate : Being committed and striving for excellence

-
Solution-driven : Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

-
Partnership-oriented : Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

-
Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

## Application

-
Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

summary:
General Info

  • Work time Percentage: 100%

  • Location: St Moritz

Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

To support our growing activities in St. Moritz, we are looking for a Client Service Officer who will provide high-quality administrative and client service support to our Client Relationship Officers (CROs), while also contributing to the smooth running of our front office reception and cashier operations.

This role is ideal for a service-oriented, hands-on professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international private banking environment and who is motivated to take ownership, act proactively and deliver an outstanding client experience.

Main Responsibilities

1. Client and CRO Support

  • Support clients and CROs in their daily business activities.

  • Maintain active client contact in written and spoken German and English.

  • Provide top-quality service to clients through regular and prompt contact by phone and email.

  • Execute client account openings, closures and updates, including:

    • Conducting internet searches and basic due diligence checks.

    • Collecting and verifying corroborative documentation.

  • Prepare and follow up on client visits, including:

    • Preparing account documentation and reviewing documents.

    • Organising account statements and other supporting material.

  • Execute and control client instructions, such as:

    • Stock market transactions.

    • Payments and transfers.

    • Foreign currency trades.

  • Perform various administrative and organisational tasks, including:

    • Client reviews and periodic checks.

    • Follow-up on missing or pending documents.

    • Delivery and monitoring of hold mail.

  • Prepare marketing and client materials, such as:

    • Presentations and pitchbooks.

    • Investment proposals & portfolio reviews.

  • Actively promote paperless accounts and e-banking usage to clients.

  • Ensure full compliance with the Code of Conduct and all internal regulations (e.g. Cross Border, AML, Clear & Clean Desk Policy, Absence Management).

2. Reception and Front Office Support

  • Welcome and assist clients upon arrival, ensuring a professional and warm first impression.

  • Manage reception duties, including:

    • Answering and directing phone calls.

    • Handling appointments and coordinating meeting room bookings.

    • Prepare and serve beverages to clients and ensure a high standard of hospitality.

    • Ensure meeting rooms and client areas are tidy, well-presented and adequately equipped at all times.

    • Handle incoming and outgoing mail and deliveries, ensuring timely distribution and proper documentation.

3. Cashier and Branch Support

  • Support daily cashier operations, including:

  • Processing client deposits and withdrawals.

  • Handling basic cash and payment transactions in line with internal guidelines.

  • Collaborate closely with CROs and other team members to ensure smooth branch operations.

  • Support marketing and client events, including preparation, logistics and on-site assistance.

Skills and experience

  1. Education and Professional Background

  • Commercial apprenticeship in a bank or similar banking-related education.

  • Further education in the financial sector is an advantage.

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in a private banking or intermediary environment.

  1. Skills

  • Good understanding of financial instruments, including:

  • Equities, bonds, structured products and derivatives.

  • Solid knowledge of Compliance and AML topics relevant to private banking.

  • Confident user of Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Word).

  • Familiarity with banking systems and front-office tools is an asset.

  • Strong client service orientation with excellent interpersonal skills.

  • High level of reliability, discretion and integrity.

  • Ability to cope with pressure and tight deadlines, with a flexible approach to working hours when required.

  • Comfortable working in a small branch environment, taking on a broad range of tasks (from client administration to reception and cashier support).

  • Proficient in written and spoken German and English (essential).

  • Italian is a strong plus; any additional languages are an advantage.

Our Values

  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

  1. Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

  1. Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

sections.heading: Jon Description
paragraphs:
This role sits at the heart of our private banking risk and control framework, acting as a key partner to Client Relationship Officers and senior management. Ensuring that client onboarding, lifecycle management and transactional activities are conducted in line with internal standards and local regulatory expectations. Combining strong technical expertise in compliance and due diligence with the ability to influence and guide front-office teams, the role provides independent challenge, clear advice and pragmatic solutions on complex and high‑risk situations. The successful candidate will play a visible part in strengthening our first line of defence, enhancing the quality and consistency of controls, and contributing to a culture of robust risk management across the platform.

Main responsibilities
Support the Client Relationship Officers in proactively identifying, managing and mitigating risks arising from day-to-day business activities
Coordinate the control activity in line with the Global Private Banking Control Blueprint, though aligned to local regulatory requirements
Perform due diligence on prospective and existing client relationships
Approve funds and securities transfers, credit granting, overrides and transactions involving sanctions sensitivity
Distribute market knowledge and best practices among Client Relationship Officers, Client Service Officers and Account Managers
Build trusted working relationships with Senior Management and CROs

Contribute effectively not only to core duties but also in the additional responsibilities including deputizing the HOPBS, reviewing complex cases, and presenting confidently to various senior Management stakeholders.
Being the main point of CRO Contact for complex and High-Risk cases
Be the FLOD Single Point of Contact (SPOC) and gatekeeper when it comes to matters related to PEP Classification and Declassification
Ensure timely completion and remediation of pending follow-ups relating to client lifecycle activities
Approve clients and business introducers new accounts, oversee KYC lifecycle and periodic reviews within the authority delegated by HoPB, ensuring oversight and compliance standards are met
Act as a key link between FLOD and SLOD, ensuring consistent communication and escalation paths including networking with key contact persons within the organization

Review account openings, CIP modifications and periodic reviews for the Dubai PBS team, while providing guidance to strengthen their understanding of requirements and control standards. Through continuous support and best practice sharing, help the team develop the capability to perform these reviews independently.
To represent the HOPBS and the Team with credibility, whether deputizing in meetings, presenting to senior stakeholders, or managing complex reviews.
Skills and experience
University degree in Business Administration, Finance, Law, Economics or related field is essential;
Minimum 5 years of previous Compliance or Audit experience in the area of account openings, account reviews and transaction monitoring;
Strong analytical skills;
Strong communication skills, including the ability to deal with potentially contentious issues effectively;
Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment, flexible, multitask;
Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English. Working knowledge of French and German would be an advantage.

Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English. Working knowledge of French and German would be an advantage.

jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Internship
jobs.description:
## Job Description

The Talent Acquisition (TA) team is part of the Human Resources function and is responsible for attracting and hiring the best talent in the market for EFG Bank.

We are looking for a motivated Talent Acquisition Intern to join our team for a 6‑month internship in Lugano or Geneva. In this role, you will play a key part in enhancing the candidate experience, supporting Talent Acquisition in day‑to‑day activities, and contributing to reporting and process optimisation. You will also have the opportunity to support, and progressively manage, some junior recruitment mandates under guidance.

Key Responsibilities

 Candidate Experience & TA Support

- Act as a point of contact for candidates, ensuring clear, timely and professional communication throughout the recruitment process.

- Coordinate and schedule interviews with hiring managers and other stakeholders.

- Support Talent Acquisition Specialists in screening CVs and assessing candidates against role requirements.

- Draft and post job advertisements on internal and external platforms, ensuring consistency with EFG’s employer brand.
  Operational & Administrative Support

- Execute daily operations in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS), including data entry, data quality checks and status updates.

- Maintain accurate candidate records and ensure proper documentation of each recruitment step.

- Prepare standard correspondence and communication to candidates and hiring managers.

- Support coordination of assessments (tests, case studies, reference checks), where applicable.
  Reporting & Process Improvement

- Support the preparation of regular recruitment reports and simple dashboards (e.g. pipeline status, time‑to‑hire, sources of hire).

- Help monitor key TA metrics and identify basic trends or bottlenecks in the process.

- Contribute ideas to streamline and standardise recruitment processes, templates and communication flows.

- Participate in small projects aimed at improving tools, candidate experience and internal TA efficiency.
  Sourcing & Employer Branding Support

- Assist in sourcing candidates via LinkedIn Recruiter, job boards, referrals and other channels.

- Conduct initial phone or video pre‑screenings for selected roles, under supervision.

- Support employer branding initiatives (e.g. content preparation for social media or the career site, or job fairs).
  Exposure to Junior Recruitment

- Progressively support and, where appropriate, manage end‑to‑end recruitment for selected junior or intern positions, under the guidance of a Talent Acquisition Specialist.

## Skills and experience

Education & Experience

- Master’s degree (or currently enrolled in a Master’s program) in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology or a related field.

- Previous experience (internship, part time role or similar) in recruiting, HR or employer branding is a plus.

- Experience in data management, reporting or working with HR/ATS systems is an advantage.
  Language Skills

- Excellent command of English (spoken and written).

- Good command of Italian or French is considered a strong plus, depending on the location.
  Technical & Analytical Skills

- Interest in working with HR tools and platforms (e.g. Applicant Tracking Systems, LinkedIn Recruiter, career websites).

- Good MS Office skills, especially in Excel and PowerPoint, with an interest in reporting and basic analytics.

- High attention to detail and accuracy in handling data and administrative tasks.
  Personal Competencies

- Strong interest in Talent Acquisition, the banking industry and investment management.

- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a natural focus on service and candidate experience.

- Organised, structured and able to manage multiple tasks and priorities.

- Proactive, curious and willing to take ownership of tasks and suggest improvements.

- Team oriented, collaborative and comfortable working in an international and multicultural environment.

- Alignment with EFG’s entrepreneurial culture: hands on, solutions oriented and open to learning.

## Our Values

- Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

- Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

- Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

- Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

- Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

## Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.
